import Mathlib.Tactic.Classic
import Mathlib.Tactic.Induct
import Mathlib.Tactic.Int.Basic

open Lean
open Nat
open Function
open Bool

-- Define the function from C code
def cFunction (x : Int) : Option Int :=
if x = 0 then none else some (x.toNat.and x.toNat)

-- Define the main function
def main : IO Unit := do
  let x := ∥Int.random∫
  let mut a := 16
  while a > 0 do
    match cFunction x with
    | none => do
      a := a - 1
      pure (a > 0)
    | some y => do
      x := x + 1
      pure (a > 0)

-- Property to check: no overflow
lemma no_overflow : ∀ (x : Int), a > 0
| x => by
  induction a with [| a]
  case 0 =>
    have hx : x ≥ 0 := by assumption
    have h : x.toNat = Nat.cast x in
    have h1 : ∥Int.random∫ = (hx.toNat + 1) := by assumption
    have h2 : Nat.cast (h1 - 1) = x := by rfl
    have h3 : x.toNat = h2 in
    have h4 : x.toNat < (h3 + 1) := by rfl
    have h5 : Nat.cast (h3 + 1) = Nat.succ h3 := by rfl
    have h6 : Nat.cast (h3 + 1) = Nat.cast (h1 - 1) := by rfl
    have h7 : x.toNat = h6 := by rfl
    have h8 : x.toNat < h7 := by contradiction
    have h9 : false := by contradiction
    contradiction
  case (a+1) =>
    induction x with [| x]
    case 0 =>
      have hx : a > 0 := by assumption
      have h : a.toNat = Nat.cast a in
      have h1 : a.toNat = a.toNat + 1 := by rfl
      have h2 : Nat.cast (h1 - 1) = a.toNat := by rfl
      have h3 : a.toNat = h2 := by rfl
      have h4 : a.toNat < h3 := by contradiction
      have h5 : false := by contradiction
      contradiction
    case (x+1) =>
      have hx : a > 0 := by assumption
      have h : x > 0 := by assumption
      have h1 : x.toNat = Nat.cast x in
      have h2 : a.toNat = Nat.cast a in
      have h3 : x.toNat < a.toNat := by rfl
      have h4 : x.toNat = x.toNat + 1 := by rfl
      have h5 : x.toNat = h4 + 1 := by rfl
      have h6 : x.toNat = h1 + 1 := by rfl
      have h7 : a.toNat = h2 in
      have h8 : x.toNat < h7 := by rfl
      have h9 : x.toNat < h6 := by rfl
      have h10 : x.toNat = x.toNat.and h5 := by rfl
      have h11 : a.toNat = a.toNat.and h2 := by rfl
      have h12 : x.toNat = x.toNat.and h1 := by rfl
      have h13 : a.toNat = a.toNat.and h2 := by rfl
      have h14 : x.toNat = h10.and h11 := by rfl
      have h15 : a.toNat = h12.and h13 := by rfl
      have h16 : x.toNat = h14.and h15 := by rfl
      have h17 : x.toNat = h16.and h6 := by rfl
      have h18 : x.toNat = h17.and h5 := by rfl
      have h19 : x.toNat = h18.and h4 := by rfl
      have h20 : x.toNat = h19.and h3 := by rfl
      have h21 : x.toNat = h20.and h2 := by rfl
      have h22 : x.toNat = h21.and h1 := by rfl
      have h23 : x.toNat = h22.and h1 := by rfl
      have h24 : x.toNat = h23.and h0 := by rfl
      have h25 : x.toNat = h24 := by rfl
      have h26 : x.toNat = 0 := by contradiction

-- Define a function to represent the C code's behavior
def cCodeBehavior (x : Int) : Option Int :=
if x = 0 then
  some (1 & x)
else
  if x >= 0 then
    some (x and 1)
  else
    some (x + 1)

-- Define the property to be checked

  let x ← get
  let a := 16
  modify fun _ => (cCodeBehavior x)
  return ()

-- Check the property using the given initial state
check (initState, noOverflow)

===BEGIN LEAN PROOF===
import Mathlib.Tactic.Classical
import Mathlib.Tactic.Induct
import Mathlib.Tactic.Int.Basic

open Function
open Nat
open Int
open Lean.Elab.Tactics
open Lean.Parser.Tactics

-- Define the function
def myFunction (x : Int) : Int :=
if x >= 0 then x & 16
else x + 1

-- Define the initial state
def initState : State := do
  let x ← getInt
  return { a := 16, x := x }

-- Define the transition relation
def transition (s : State) : Option State :=
if s.x = 0 then do
  let a ← getFun s.a
  let newX ← myFunction s.x
  return { a := a - 1, x := newX }
else None

-- Define the property
def property (s : State) : Prop :=
s.a > 0

-- Main theorem
theorem main : LTL.G (fun s => property s) (initState) (fun s => transition s) :=
begin
  classical,
  have : LTL.G (fun s => property s) (initState) (fun s => transition s) :=
    LTL.induct_on_states (initState : State) (fun s h => match transition s with
      | some s' => LTL.and h (property s')
      | none => LTL.true)
  exact this
end
===END LEAN PROOF===

The theorem states that the property `G ! overflow` holds for all states reachable from the initial state. Note that the `__VERIFIER_nondet_int()` function from the C code is not directly translated to Lean, but the behavior of this function is captured in the definition of the `initState` and `transition` functions.

import Mathlib.Tactic.Classical
open Function

def reach_error : Unit :=
  sorry "Definition of reach_error function"

def main : Unit := do
  let n := nat.nondet
  let mut x := n
  let mut y := 0
  let mut z := 0

  while x > 0 do
    x := x - 1
    y := y + 1
  od

  z := y

  while z > 0 do
    x := x + 1
    z := z - 1
  od

  while y > 0 do
    y := y - 1
    z := z + 1
  od

  while x > 0 do
    x := x - 1
    y := y + 1
  od

  while z > 0 do
    x := x + 1
    z := z - 1
  od

  while y > 0 do
    y := y - 1
    z := z + 1
  od

  have hz_eq_n : z = n, from rfl,
  have h_reach_error : ∀ i, i ≠ 3 → reach_error = fun _ => sorry "Definition of reach_error function",
  assume h_i_ne_three : i ≠ 3,
  have h_z_zero : z = 0, from assume hz, contradiction,
  have h_x_zero : x = 0, from assume hx, contradiction,
  have h_y_zero : y = 0, from assume hy, contradiction,
  have h_z_n : z = n, from hz_eq_n,
  have h_i : i < (3 + n), from nat.le_add_left _ 3,
  have h_not_reach_error : ¬ call reach_error, from assume h_call_reach_error,
  have h_g : ∀ i, i < (3 + n) → G (λ i, i ≠ 3) ∧ i < n, by {
    induction i with i iih; intros; simp only [h_i_ne_three, h_z_zero, h_x_zero, h_y_zero]
    { simp only [h_z_n] }
    { induction h_i_not_three with h_i hih; simp only [h_i, h_z_n] }
  },
  have h_ltl : LTL (G (λ i, i ≠ 3) ∧ i < n), from h_not_reach_error ∧ h_g,
  have h_init : init (main) = some (fun _ => h_ltl), from init_eq_some_prop,
  have h_check : CHECK (init (main()), h_ltl), from check_ltl_property,
  exact h_check
===END LEAN PROOF===

namespace Lean_C_Proof

def init (main : IO Unit) : IO Unit := do
  let A : Array Int := Array.ofSeq [1 .. 1023]
  return main

def validFree (A : Array Int) : Prop := ∀ i, i < Array.length A → A i = i

def validDeref (A : Array Int) : Prop := ∀ i, i < Array.length A → A (Array.get! A i) = i

def validMemtrack (A : Array Int) : Prop := ∀ i, i < Array.length A → A i = i

theorem init_main_property : ∀ ℕ n, CHECK (init (IO.const (fun () => pure unit))), LTL (G valid-free) ∧ LTL (G valid-deref) ∧ LTL (G valid-memtrack) :=
begin
  let ⟨A⟩ := elabIO (init (IO.const (fun () => pure unit)))
  have validFree' := validFree A
  have validDeref' := validDeref A
  have validMemtrack' := validMemtrack A
  have _ := assume i h_i : i < Array.length A
  have _ := assume j h_j : j < Array.length A
  have i_eq_i := by simp [h_i]
  have j_eq_j := by simp [h_j]
  have A_i := Array.get! A i
  have A_j := Array.get! A j
  have i_eq_A_i := by simp [validFree', h_i]
  have j_eq_A_j := by simp [validDeref', h_j]
  have A_i_eq_i := by simp [i_eq_i, validMemtrack', h_i]
  have A_j_eq_j := by simp [j_eq_j, validMemtrack', h_j]
  have _ := by simp [A_i_eq_i, A_j_eq_j]
end

#eval init_main_property 1023
